reportSummary.executiveSummary: For 2026-04-18 to 2026-04-24, the account spent 4,243.77 and generated 320,745 impressions, 76,334 reach, 7,695 clicks, a 2.4% CTR, $0.55 CPC, and 4.2 frequency. Overall delivery shows solid click volume with moderate cost efficiency, while performance varies meaningfully across campaigns.
whatChanged: Top actions were page_engagement (24,068), post_engagement (24,060), video_view (16,347), link_click (7,405), and landing_page_view (5,617).
Among the top campaigns, CTR ranged from 0.78% to 6.34%, and CPC ranged from $0.33 to $1.24, indicating uneven efficiency across spend.
The strongest CTRs in the top campaigns were 6.34% and 5.30%, while two campaigns were below 0.80% CTR.
Campaign spend was relatively concentrated, with the top five campaigns accounting for most of the reported spend in this snapshot.
risks: Two top campaigns show low CTR and higher CPC relative to the better-performing campaigns, which may be dragging overall efficiency.
Frequency of 4.2 suggests audiences may be seeing ads multiple times, so creative fatigue should be monitored.
Because only snapshot data is provided, there is no prior-period comparison here to confirm trend direction.
nextActions: Review the two lowest-CTR campaigns and test new creative or audience refinements.
Shift budget toward the campaigns with 5.30% and 6.34% CTR if downstream quality is acceptable.
Check landing page experience for the 5,617 landing_page_view actions to ensure clicks are converting into site visits as expected.
Monitor frequency and creative performance to avoid further efficiency decline.
reportSummary.slackMessage: Weekly snapshot: spend 4,243.77 drove 320,745 impressions, 7,695 clicks, 2.4% CTR, and $0.55 CPC. Best campaign CTRs were 6.34% and 5.30%; two campaigns were below 0.80% CTR and should be reviewed.
